footer.description: A collaborative India-Netherlands initiative to build a low-cost, multi-hazard early warning system for communities at risk.
footer.copyright: © 2025 LODESTAR Project Consortium | Funded by DST (India) & NWO (Netherlands)

notfound.subtitle: Lost Signal
notfound.text: You've navigated to a data point that is off our forecast map. The requested page could not be found. Let's get you back to charted territory.

hero.subtitle: Resilience • Intelligence • Community
hero.description: A next-generation multi-hazard early warning system bridging the gap between advanced predictive analytics and community-led action.

ews.title: Early Warning System
ews.text: Developing a multi-hazard EWS for both single and compound events.

labs.title: Living Labs
labs.text: Co-creating solutions through participatory workshops with citizens and experts.

science.title: Citizen Science
science.text: Integrating local knowledge and real-time, user-submitted data into scientific models.

disaster.title: Disaster Management
disaster.text: Enhancing the resilience of at-risk communities against extreme climate events.

cta.title: Live Geo-Dashboard
cta.description: Access real-time active alerts, flood forecasts, and community reports in our 3D interactive map interface.

about.title: About The LODESTAR Project
about.subtitle: Understanding the vision, objectives, and methodology behind our collaborative effort to build disaster resilience.

challenge.title: The Challenge
challenge.text: Existing Early Warning Systems often lack sufficient lead times and fail to integrate crucial community knowledge. This creates a "know-do gap," where warnings don't translate into effective local actions.

vision.title: Our Vision
vision.text: LODESTAR's vision is to co-create a low-cost, multi-hazard EWS using a "living labs" approach. This makes the system more comprehensive, participatory, and responsive, improving disaster preparedness for all.

objective1.title: Objective 1
objective1.text: To develop socio-technical disaster and emergency (early warning) services (MH-EWS dashboards) to address the challenges of extreme compound disaster events in India and the Netherlands.

objective2.title: Objective 2
objective2.text: To improve flood and drought modelling and user interface by focusing on innovative technologies, to arrive at an integrated MH-EWS for floods and/or droughts.

objective3.title: Objective 3
objective3.text: To develop socio-cultural and management innovations via living labs, necessary for implementing new technologies and accelerating uptake, while ensuring long-term sustainability.

ws1.title: WS 1: Disaster Context & Knowledge Gaps
ws1.text: Sets the disaster context by taking stock of existing systems and identifying socio-technical knowledge gaps in flood and drought management to build a foundation for the project.

ws2.title: WS 2: Real-time Data Assimilation
ws2.text: Conducts real-time hydrometeorological data assimilation using computer vision-based algorithms and extended weather forecasts, leveraging low-cost sensors and citizen science.

ws3.title: WS 3: Citizen Science & Living Labs
ws3.text: Creates synergy between citizens, academics, and professionals using 'living labs' and 'serious games' to facilitate knowledge exchange, co-create solutions, and ensure the EWS meets user needs.

ws4.title: WS 4: Integrated Dashboard & Forecasting
ws4.text: Develops a multi-hazard forecast framework using an ensemble of models (AI/ML, process-based) to co-create a user-centric, integrated MH-EWS dashboard for mobile and web.

ws5.title: WS 5: Capacity Building & Internationalization
ws5.text: Builds the disaster management capacity of all partners and external stakeholders in India and the Netherlands through training, workshops, summer academies, and international outreach.

alerts.title: LODESTAR Alert Center
alerts.subtitle: A centralized hub for real-time, multi-hazard early warnings for our study sites in India and the Netherlands.
alerts.no_alerts: No active alerts match your current filter settings.

list.g1: Brahmaputra river has crossed the warning level due to heavy upstream rainfall.
list.g1_a: Evacuate low-lying areas near the riverbank. Move valuables to higher ground. Follow instructions from ASDMA.
list.a1: Below-average monsoon rainfall continues. Soil moisture is critically low.
list.a1_a: Implement water conservation measures. Farmers are advised to consider drought-resistant crops.
list.b1: Intense short-duration rainfall expected, leading to high risk of urban waterlogging.
list.b1_a: Avoid travel in low-lying areas and underpasses. Ensure storm drains are clear of debris.
list.d1: River levels are elevated due to North Sea tidal surge but are expected to remain below flood stage.
list.d1_a: Monitor water levels and be aware of potential for minor coastal flooding.
list.g2: Compound Event: Fluvial flooding from the river is now combined with pluvial (rainfall) flooding, overwhelming city drainage.
list.g2_a: This is a high-risk situation. Seek shelter on higher floors or designated evacuation centers immediately.

livingLabs.title: Living Labs & Citizen Science
livingLabs.subtitle: Where community knowledge meets scientific innovation. This is the heart of LODESTAR's bottom-up approach to building disaster resilience.

whatIs.title: What is a Living Lab?
whatIs.text: A Living Lab is an open innovation environment where we co-create solutions in real-life contexts. It's a space where we bring together all stakeholders to ensure our Multi-Hazard Early Warning System (MH-EWS) matches the actual needs, cultures, and creative potentials of the end-users. This approach moves beyond traditional top-down systems to bridge the critical 'know-do' gap.

mapping.title: Participatory Social Mapping
mapping.text: We begin by mapping the social landscape of disaster risk. Through collaborative exercises, we identify 'Who knows what?' and 'Who needs to know what?' to ensure our tools address the right problems for the right people.

gaming.title: Serious Gaming & Scenarios
gaming.text: Interactive and immersive games built on realistic disaster scenarios are used to elicit tacit knowledge, test response strategies, and encourage creative problem-solving among diverse stakeholders.

learning.title: Horizontal Learning
learning.text: We foster an environment of peer-to-peer knowledge exchange, connecting citizens, experts, and officials. This breaks down hierarchies and ensures that all forms of knowledge are valued equally.

cta.title: Become a Citizen Scientist
cta.subtitle: You can play a direct role in making your community safer. Here’s how:

data.title: Contribute Real-time Data
data.text: Use our upcoming mobile tools to share vital on-the-ground information like water levels.

workshop.title: Join a Workshop
workshop.text: Participate in our local Living Labs to share your experiences and test new tools.

knowledge.title: Share Local Knowledge
knowledge.text: Your knowledge of past events is invaluable for building more accurate models.

seriousGames.title: Serious Games Hub
seriousGames.subtitle: Dive straight into playable games that explore disaster risk, resilience and strategic response.
seriousGames.sectionTitle: Play These Real-World Disaster Risk Games

stopDisasters.title: Stop Disasters! (UNDRR)
stopDisasters.blurb: A multi-hazard simulation by UNDRR: protect cities from tsunami, earthquake, flood, wildfire and cyclone. Play online and test your risk-management strategy.

disasterMaster.title: Disaster Master (Ready.gov)
disasterMaster.blurb: Youth-friendly game: face various disasters, build readiness and resilience through levels of play.

buildKit.title: Build a Kit Game (Ready.gov)
buildKit.blurb: Interactive game where you build your emergency kit and choose how to act when disasters strike.

disasterResponse.title: The Disaster Response Game (ISR/NYAS)
disasterResponse.blurb: Digital scenario game: wildfires, hurricanes, food-security crises. Hone rapid decision-making skills under pressure.

research.title: Research & Knowledge Hub
research.subtitle: Access the project's deliverables, key publications, open data repositories, and foundational documents.

a4eu.title: Pan-European Decision-Support Dashboard (A4EU)
a4eu.summary: A key dashboard from the ANYWHERE project developed by Wageningen University, serving as a model for LODESTAR's EWS.

games.title: Serious Games Catalogue & Facilitation Manual
games.summary: A portfolio of physical and digital serious games with a manual for developing and facilitating them in stakeholder workshops.

storm.title: Calibrated Hydrological Model (STORM V1)
storm.summary: A calibrated urban flood model for Bangalore, previously used for crowdsourcing studies and foundational to this project's modelling work.

subcultures.title: Flood disaster subcultures in The Netherlands
subcultures.summary: Examines the social and cultural responses to flood risks in Dutch communities, providing crucial context for the project's socio-technical approach.

interface.title: Science-policy interface on water scarcity in India
interface.summary: Investigates the communication and knowledge gaps between scientific research and policy-making regarding water scarcity in India.

forecasts.title: Moving from drought hazard to drought impact forecasts
forecasts.summary: A foundational paper on advancing drought EWS from simply predicting hazards to forecasting their tangible, real-world impacts on communities.

openScience.title: Data, Models & Open Science
openScience.text: LODESTAR is committed to the FAIR (Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, Reusable) guiding principles for scientific data management. We aim to make knowledge creation, transfer, and exchange transparent and interactive.

cards.fourTu: Processed data used for publications will be stored and made available via the 4TU research data centre.
cards.github: Algorithms, scripts, and software developed during the project will be shared through platforms like GitHub.

sendai.title: Sendai Framework
sendai.text: The project's goals align with this key framework for Disaster Risk Reduction.

team.title: The LODESTAR Consortium
team.subtitle: A detailed overview of the dedicated, transdisciplinary team driving the project.

m1.name: Professor Anamika Barua
m1.title: Professor, Dept. of Humanities and Social Sciences
m1.institutionContext: As one of India's top universities and a leading institute in engineering and disaster management, IIT-G provides the project's anchor in India.
m1.projectContribution: Leads the Indian consortium, provides overall project co-ordination, supervises PhD students, and is directly involved in Work Streams 1, 3 & 5.

publication.title: Media reporting on conflicts and cooperation... for the Brahmaputra basin.
publication.relevance: This research provides critical insight into the socio-political context of water management, essential for the success of a cross-community EWS.

m2.name: Dr. Jeroen F. Warner
m2.title: Associate Professor, Crisis and Disaster Studies
m2.institutionContext: A world-leading institution in life sciences and social sciences, WUR brings interdisciplinary expertise in water, climate, and disaster studies.
m2.projectContribution: Leads the Dutch consortium, provides overall project co-ordination, supervises PhDs and is directly involved in Work Streams 3 & 5.

publication.title: The politics of adaptive climate management: Scientific recipes and lived reality.
publication.relevance: This work is foundational to LODESTAR’s core mission, exploring the crucial intersection of scientific solutions and the lived experiences of at-risk communities.

internal.drive_subtitle: Secure administrative gateway to LODESTAR's modeling data and SFTP services.
internal.enter_credentials: Enter your internal credentials to proceed.
internal.note_text: This portal is intended for authorized internal personnel only. Access to modeling data and internal SFTP services is restricted to consortium members.
